reasoning: Butterfly is a rigid property (+R) because anything that is a butterfly must be one essentially - an individual cannot cease being a butterfly and remain the same individual (it cannot become a caterpillar again, for instance). It carries identity (+I) and supplies its own identity criterion (+O) as a biological species/natural kind, with identity determined by its biological constitution and developmental stage. It has unity (+U) as butterflies are intrinsic wholes - integrated organisms with functionally interdependent parts (wings, body, organs). It is independent (-D) as a butterfly does not intrinsically depend on other entities for its existence (though it may have causal dependencies, these are not ontological dependencies). As a rigid property that supplies its own identity criterion, Butterfly qualifies as a Sortal, specifically a Natural Kind within biological taxonomy.

Red is a color quality/attribute. **Rigidity (-R)**: An object can be red at one time and not red at another (e.g., a painted wall can be repainted), so it's non-rigid - not essential to all instances in all worlds. **Identity (-I, -O)**: Red does not carry or supply identity conditions; we don't identify objects as 'the red one' in an absolute sense, and color properties don't provide criteria for counting or individuating entities. **Unity (-U)**: Red can apply to scattered or non-whole entities (e.g., 'red things in a room' form no intrinsic whole); the property itself doesn't unify instances into wholes. **Dependence (+D)**: Red cannot exist independently - it must inhere in some substrate (a surface, object, or material). As a quality, it's ontologically dependent on its bearer. Red is best classified as a quality or attribute (non-sortal), potentially a Mixin in some ontological frameworks, as it can apply contingently across different kinds of entities without providing identity or unity.

Food is **-R (Non-Rigid)** because an entity is not essentially food across all possible worlds - a tomato can exist without being food (e.g., as a decorative object, or before being designated for consumption). It's **+I (Carries Identity)** and **+O (Supplies Own Identity)** because food items have their own identity conditions based on their material constitution and physical boundaries (this apple, that loaf of bread). Food is **-U (Non-Unifying)** because food can be scattered or composite (a meal, groceries, a pantry of food) without requiring intrinsic wholeness - we can speak of 'some food' or 'all the food' without topological unity. Finally, food is **-D (Independent)** as food substances can exist without necessarily depending on other entities - though they may depend on consumers functionally, they don't have intrinsic existential dependence. Food functions as a non-rigid sortal category that substances can enter and exit based on their intended or actual use for consumption.

Animal is a rigid property (+R) because any instance that is an animal is essentially an animal in all possible worlds - a dog cannot cease to be an animal and remain the same individual. It carries identity (+I) and supplies its own identity condition (+O) as a natural kind, providing criteria for recognizing the same animal over time through biological continuity. Animal exhibits unity (+U) as instances are intrinsic wholes - an animal is not merely a sum of parts but an integrated biological organism. It is independent (-D) as animals exist as autonomous substances without intrinsic existential dependence on other entities. This makes Animal a substance sortal and natural kind in the ontological taxonomy.

RedApple is anti-rigid (~R) because an apple can become red (e.g., through ripening) and can cease to be red (e.g., through decay or painting) while remaining the same apple - being red is not essential across all possible worlds. It carries identity (+I) inherited from 'Apple', which as a natural kind supplies identity conditions for physical objects. However, RedApple does not supply its own identity condition (-O); the identity comes from being an apple, not from being red. It has unity (+U) because each red apple is an intrinsic whole - a complete, integrated physical object. It is independent (-D) as red apples do not intrinsically depend on other entities for their existence. This classifies RedApple as a Phase - a temporal, contingent state of apples characterized by the property of being red.

Group is a non-rigid sortal that carries and supplies its own identity condition. It is -R because entities can enter and leave group membership without losing their individual identity (e.g., a person can be part of a group temporarily). It is +I and +O because groups have their own identity conditions based on membership criteria, composition, or purpose that distinguish one group from another. It is -U (non-unifying) because groups are collections of members that don't necessarily form intrinsic wholes - members retain their individual boundaries and the group is defined by the relationship among members rather than being a unified whole. It is -D (independent) because a group can exist as a distinct entity without necessarily depending on any particular external entity, though it does require members (but these are internal to the group concept, not external dependencies).

Country is +R (Rigid) because something that is a country must be essentially a country - if an entity ceases to be a country (e.g., through dissolution or merger), it ceases to exist as that entity; it's not merely losing a phase or role. Country is +I (Carries Identity) and +O (Supplies Own Identity) because it provides its own criteria for identifying and distinguishing one country from another (typically through sovereign territory, government, population, and international recognition). These are intrinsic identity conditions specific to countries. Country is +U (Unifying) because each country forms an intrinsic whole - a unified geopolitical entity with boundaries, governance structures, and territorial integrity that make it a complete unit rather than an arbitrary collection. Country is -D (Independent) because a country exists as a sovereign, self-standing entity; while it may have diplomatic relations and dependencies in practice, ontologically it does not intrinsically require another specific entity for its existence. This makes Country a Sortal - specifically a substantial kind that carries its own identity principle and applies rigidly to its instances.

AmountOfMatter is rigid (+R) because something that is an amount of matter is essentially so across all possible worlds - it cannot cease being an amount of matter while continuing to exist. It carries identity (+I) and supplies its own identity condition (+O) based on material composition and quantity - what makes this particular amount of matter distinct from another is its specific material makeup and extent. It is non-unifying (-U) because amounts of matter do not necessarily form intrinsic wholes; they can be arbitrary quantities or scattered portions without inherent boundaries (e.g., 'the water in this ocean' or 'the gold in this mine'). It is independent (-D) as amounts of matter can exist without intrinsically depending on other entities. This classifies as a substance sortal or base category that provides fundamental identity criteria for material entities.

PhysicalObject is +R (Rigid) because being a physical object is essential to all its instances in all possible worlds - if something is a physical object, it cannot cease to be one while continuing to exist. It carries +I (Identity) and +O (Own Identity) because physical objects have intrinsic identity conditions based on their spatio-temporal continuity and material constitution. PhysicalObject is +U (Unifying) as instances are intrinsic wholes with clear boundaries and structural unity distinguishing them from mere aggregates. It is -D (Independent) because physical objects exist autonomously without intrinsic dependence on other entities for their existence. This combination (+R, +O, +U, -D) classifies PhysicalObject as a Sortal, specifically a Natural Kind that provides essential properties and identity criteria for its instances.

GroupOfPeople is non-rigid (-R) because being part of a group is not essential to the individual people in all possible worlds - the same persons could exist without being in that particular group. It carries identity (+I) and supplies its own identity condition (+O) based on the criterion that defines group membership (e.g., specific member composition, organizational rules, or shared purpose). It has anti-unity (~U) because a group is inherently an aggregate/collection of discrete individuals rather than an intrinsic whole - it's a sum of parts without intrinsic wholeness. It is dependent (+D) because a group cannot exist without its constituent members - the group's existence intrinsically depends on the people who compose it. The non-rigid nature combined with supplying identity makes this a Category (a non-rigid property that carries identity criteria but allows instances to gain/lose the property).

LivingBeing is a rigid property (+R) because being alive is essential to all its instances across all possible worlds - an entity cannot be a living being in one world and not in another while remaining the same entity. It carries identity (+I) and supplies its own identity condition (+O) through biological criteria (e.g., genetic continuity, metabolic processes, organizational closure). Living beings exhibit unity (+U) as they are intrinsic wholes with integrated biological systems that maintain homeostasis and functional organization. They are independent (-D) as living beings do not intrinsically depend on other entities for their existence as living beings, though they may depend on environmental conditions. This classifies LivingBeing as a Natural Kind sortal, representing a fundamental category with strong ontological commitment.

SocialEntity is analyzed as follows: (1) Rigidity: -R (Non-Rigid) because entities can be social in some possible worlds but not others - a group of people might exist as mere individuals or become a social entity depending on their organization and relationships. (2) Identity: +I because social entities carry identity conditions based on their social structure, membership, and organizing principles. (3) Own Identity: +O because SocialEntity supplies its own identity criterion - what makes a social entity the same over time is determined by social factors like continuity of organization, purpose, or membership rules, distinct from mere physical or biological identity. (4) Unity: -U (Non-Unifying) because social entities vary widely in their wholeness - some (like corporations) have strong internal unity, while others (like social movements) may be loosely organized without forming intrinsic wholes. (5) Dependence: +D (Dependent) because social entities fundamentally depend on the existence and participation of individual persons or agents - they cannot exist without their members or participants, making them intrinsically dependent. As a high-level category with non-rigid identification, SocialEntity functions as a Category in the ontological taxonomy.

Location is non-rigid (-R) because entities can change their location while remaining the same entity (e.g., a person can be in different locations at different times). It carries identity (+I) and supplies its own identity condition (+O) based on spatial coordinates or reference frames - a location is identified by its position in space. Location is non-unifying (-U) as it can refer to both discrete points and extended regions without requiring intrinsic wholeness. It is independent (-D) as locations exist as part of space regardless of whether entities occupy them. As a non-rigid property with its own identity, Location functions as a Category in Guarino-Welty taxonomy - a high-level classification that can organize entities without being essential to them.

Fruit is analyzed as a biological natural kind. **Rigidity (+R)**: An entity that is a fruit is essentially a fruit in all possible worlds - an apple cannot cease being a fruit while continuing to exist as the same entity. **Identity (+I) and Own Identity (+O)**: Fruit carries and supplies its own identity condition based on biological criteria (seed-bearing structure of flowering plants). Each fruit has determinate identity conditions grounded in its biological nature. **Unity (+U)**: Individual fruits are intrinsic wholes with natural boundaries - an apple is a unified biological entity, not merely an aggregate. **Dependence (-D)**: While fruits develop from plants, once individuated, a fruit exists as an independent particular object (e.g., a picked apple continues to exist independently). This makes Fruit a rigid sortal that provides identity and unity criteria for its instances.

Vertebrate is a rigid property (+R) because having a vertebral column (backbone) is essential to all its instances across all possible worlds - an entity cannot be a vertebrate in one world and not in another while remaining the same entity. It carries identity (+I) and supplies its own identity condition (+O) as a biological natural kind, distinguished by the fundamental anatomical feature of possessing a spinal column with vertebrae. This structural criterion provides a clear principle of identity and individuation. Vertebrate has unity (+U) because each instance is an intrinsic whole - an individual organism with integrated biological systems organized around the vertebral column. It is independent (-D) as vertebrate organisms exist as autonomous biological entities without intrinsic existential dependence on other specific entities. As a rigid, identity-supplying natural kind with unity, Vertebrate classifies as a Natural Kind in the Guarino-Welty taxonomy.

A caterpillar is a temporal phase in the life cycle of a lepidopteran (butterfly or moth). It is ~R (Anti-Rigid) because being a caterpillar is a temporary, essential property during that developmental stage—no individual can be a caterpillar in all possible worlds or times, and it's essential to the phase that it will cease being a caterpillar (metamorphosis). It carries identity (+I) inherited from the biological organism it is part of, but does not supply its own identity condition (-O), as identity is provided by the underlying organism. Caterpillars are unifying (+U) as they are complete, integrated biological organisms with intrinsic wholeness during that phase. They are independent (-D) as they exist as autonomous organisms without requiring external entities for their existence (though they need food, this is not intrinsic ontological dependence). Classification: Phase—a transient stage with anti-rigid essence.

Person is +R (rigid) because an entity that is a person is essentially a person in all possible worlds - something cannot stop being a person and remain the same individual. It is +I (carries identity) and +O (supplies own identity) because 'Person' provides its own identity criteria based on biological/psychological continuity that distinguishes one person from another. It is +U (unifying) as persons are intrinsic wholes with clear boundaries and internal cohesion. It is -D (independent) because persons do not intrinsically depend on other entities for their existence - they are substantial entities. This makes Person a Sortal, specifically a Natural Kind, as it is a rigid property that carries its own identity condition and applies to independent, unified particulars.

LegalAgent is anti-rigid (~R) because an entity (person, organization) can gain or lose legal agency status without ceasing to exist - e.g., a person becomes a legal agent when recognized by a legal system but wasn't always one. It carries identity (+I) inherited from its foundation (Person, Organization), but doesn't supply its own identity condition (-O) - the identity comes from the underlying entity type. It is unifying (+U) as legal agents are discrete, countable wholes with clear boundaries. It is independent (-D) in the sense that while legal recognition is required for the role, the underlying entity exists independently. This is a典型 Role - a property that entities can acquire and lose while maintaining their identity, contingent upon external conditions (legal recognition/capacity).

Entity is the most general, top-level concept in ontology. It is rigid (+R) because anything that exists is necessarily an entity in all possible worlds - there is no possible world where something exists but is not an entity. However, Entity does not carry identity (-I, -O) because it is too general to provide identity conditions; identity must be supplied by more specific properties (e.g., Person, Apple). Entity is non-unifying (-U) as it encompasses both wholes and scattered aggregates without distinction. It is independent (-D) as the concept itself doesn't require dependence on other entities - it's the foundation. As a rigid property without unity criteria that subsumes both sortals and non-sortals, Entity is classified as a Category - the highest-level rigid property in the ontological hierarchy.

Organization is analyzed as follows: (1) Rigidity: +R because something that is an organization is necessarily an organization in all possible worlds where it exists - an entity cannot cease being an organization and remain the same entity. (2) Identity: +I because organizations carry identity conditions - we can distinguish one organization from another. (3) Own Identity: +O because Organization supplies its own global identity criterion based on organizational structure, purpose, and membership - these are intrinsic to what makes an organization identifiable. (4) Unity: -U because organizations are not intrinsic wholes; they are composed of separate members and resources that don't form a topologically connected unity. While they have organizational cohesion, their parts (people, assets) can be spatially distributed and are not physically unified like a physical object would be. (5) Dependence: -D because while an organization depends on its members relationally, it exists as an independent social entity once constituted - it is not intrinsically dependent in the ontological sense, as it can persist through complete member turnover. As a rigid property that supplies its own identity, Organization functions as a Sortal Category in Guarino and Welty's framework.

Agent is a rigid property (+R) because being an agent is essential to all its instances across all possible worlds - an entity that is an agent cannot cease to be an agent while remaining the same entity. It carries identity (+I) and supplies its own identity condition (+O) based on the capacity for autonomous action, intentionality, and goal-directed behavior. Agent is unifying (+U) as instances are intrinsic wholes with agentive capabilities forming a cohesive unity. It is independent (-D) because agents do not intrinsically depend on other entities to exist - they are autonomous entities. As a rigid property that supplies identity and unity, Agent qualifies as a Sortal, specifically a Kind, representing a fundamental category of entities capable of intentional action.

Apple is a rigid property (+R) because anything that is an apple is essentially an apple in all possible worlds - an apple cannot cease being an apple and remain the same individual. It carries identity (+I) and supplies its own identity condition (+O) as a natural kind with biological criteria (genetic structure, developmental origin from apple tree). Apple is unifying (+U) because each instance is an intrinsic whole - a complete fruit with integrated parts (skin, flesh, core, seeds). It is independent (-D) as apples exist as distinct physical objects without intrinsic dependence on other entities for their existence. As a natural kind that is rigid, carries its own identity, and is unifying, Apple qualifies as a Sortal.
